channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NBA The Run Release Date Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
NBA The Run is set to release in less than a month. Get ready to take it back to the streets when NBA The Run, the latest arcade basketball experience, launches on June 9 for the PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC via Steam.

The base game will be priced at $29.99, while the Deluxe Edition, which includes 3 unlocked Rookie variants and bonus in-game currency, will cost $39.99. The Deluxe Edition bonuses feature the Stephen Curry Golden State Warriors ‘09 Rookie Variant, Luka Dončić Dallas Mavericks ‘18 Rookie Variant, Kevin Durant Seattle Supersonics ‘07 Rookie Variant, and 1000 CRED, the in-game currency.

“You can use that CRED to purchase items in the Shop, including new alternate jerseys for a specific player, advanced dunk animations, taunts, badges, banners, and more. Shop purchases are simple – you get what you want, no packs to rip here,” noted a developer blog, announcing the release date.

NBA The Run Holding Closed Beta This Weekend

Additionally, it was announced that a closed beta for NBA The Run will begin this weekend, on May 16 at 1:00 PM EST. The playtest will last only three hours, but the developers say they have already gathered a ton of feedback from their original playtest less than two weeks ago.

“RUN it back with us this weekend on Saturday, May 16, from 10:00 am – 2:00 pm PST and try out street legend Spin Cycle, known for his overzealous swag and unbelievable handles, in addition to trying out new players and Knockout Solos, our mode where each player controls their whole team of 3,” noted the blog.

Learn more about that original playtest here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor NBA The Run’s development and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Are you excited for the upcoming basketball game? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1789: Hints and Answer for May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1789 for May 13, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, May 13, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1789 is challenging. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4.8 out of 6 guesses. That makes it the same as the 4.8-guess average from May 12.

General Wordle Hints For May 13

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes someone or something unfashionable or old-fashioned.
  • Hint 2: The word usually has a negative tone related to appearance or style.
  • Hint 3: You might use it when clothing looks plain or outdated.
  • Hint 4: It suggests a lack of elegance or modern flair.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1789 for May 13, 2026.

Starter Words For May 13, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • DOUBT
  • CLOUD
  • DOWEL
  • WOODY
  • HOWDY

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1789 On May 13, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: DOWDY

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

DOWDY is an adjective that means “not stylish; drab; old-fashioned.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1788 on May 12, 2026

Answer: CLOCK

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1789

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on May 14 will be Wordle Game #1790.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Sony Has Helped Fund Over 120 Projects This Generation
item.content:encoded:
Sony has stated that it has either partially or fully funded over 120 projects in this generation.

Multiple new details about Sony and PlayStation have emerged from a new interview. The VP has stated that some Chinese and Korean studios seem to move faster than those in Western and Japanese regions. The interview also covered Sony’s reasons for charging a 30% commission fee on store purchases. Now, Sony discussed its funding activities, stating it has supported over 100 games.

Sony Talks About Funding Over 120 Projects, Without Counting Hero Projects

In a recent interview with The Game Business, Christian Svensson, Sony’s VP of second and third-party content, talked about funding over 120 projects this generation. Svensson spoke about the company’s approach of giving indie games the same stage as major AAA partners. He emphasized that indies are valued equally and are seen as ‘an amazing complement to the breadth and depth story that needs to exist on our platform.’

Svensson added that he does not want to ‘diminish’ the team’s work on funding. He said Sony has “funded either wholly or partially more than 120 projects this generation.” This figure does not include Hero Projects. Svensson also mentioned that a ‘small diversity set of funds’ is issued each year, and the team manages a ‘non-recoupable indie fund’ to support indie content that would not otherwise reach PlayStation.

In other news, the VP has suggested that PlayStation would be just a plastic box without content. Additionally, Sony is currently facing a class-action lawsuit alleging it monopolized the market by restricting third-party games on its platform. What are your thoughts on Sony helping fund over 120 projects in this generation? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about David Vonderhaar’s Bulletfarm working on a new first-person multiplayer game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Marvel Rivals Devil Dinosaur Guide—Release Date, Role, Abilities, Team-Up & More
item.content:encoded:
It’s officially confirmed that Devil Dinosaur will be the first addition to Marvel Rivals Season 8, with Cyclops arriving shortly after in Season 8.5.

As is always the case, that means there’s a roster of new abilities and team-ups to familiarise yourself with, and with Devil Dinosaur being the 50th addition to the game, that’s a lot of brain power required!

Thankfully, we’ve managed to combine everything you need to know about Devil Dinosaur’s arrival in Marvel Rivals into one handy article. Read on to learn details regarding his release date, role, abilities, and more!

When Does Devil Dinosaur Come To Marvel Rivals?

Devil Dinosaur roars into Marvel Rivals alongside the beginning of Season 8, which is set to begin on May 15, 2026.

He’ll be free to play for anyone to use, as you won’t need to unlock the character; instead, he’ll be added straight into your roster. That being said, you’ll want to know where to look in the character selector, so which class is Devil Dinosaur?

What Role Is Devil Dinosaur?

Devil Dinosaur is officially a Vanguard, as the giant T-Rex will help shield teammates whilst bringing raw primal power to the roster.

Fans have long been crying out for more Vanguards to be added to the game, and it looks like their prayers might finally be answered, as Devil Dinosaur is ready to stomp onto the scene. In a world where Cyclops is also a tank, it could be a very fruitful season for Vanguard players!

All Devil Dinosaur Abilities, Explained

It seems like Devil Dinosaur will be a perfect space taker, with his abilities lending to a dominant front-line antagonist.

Perhaps his most notable ability, Devil Dinosaur can clamp and hold enemies in his jaw, thus taking them out of the fight momentarily. Partner that with his attacks dealing continuous bleed damage, as well as a mouth-fired laser for ranged foes, and he’s set to be a menace at the center of battle.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Vote During Marvel Rivals Hellfire Gala 2026

His main attacks rely on his colossal size, and he even has a forcefield shield to nullify some of the damage coming his way. As for his Ultimate, Devil Dinosaur will transform into an unstoppable behemoth, as he can charge forward and trample everyone in his way.

Full official details regarding his abilities are yet to be revealed, but as soon as they are, I’ll be sure to update this article with a full breakdown of how Devil Dinosaur plays in-game. In the meantime, check out the gameplay trailer!

Devil Dinosaur Team-Up Abilities, Explained

Devil Dinosaur will have one team-up ability at launch, and it’s with none other than the Punisher.

Named Primal Punishment, Devil Dinosaur is the anchor to the team-up. When prompted by Punisher, Devil Dinosaur can accept, allowing Punisher to ride on his back like a mount!

  • More Insider Gaming: Cyclops & Devil Dinosaur Confirmed For Marvel Rivals Season 8

Once he’s up there, Punisher can only use his normal attacks and scourge grenade, but he will receive reduced damage in return, with any excess being distributed to Devil Dinosaur. Healing is also shared between the two when Punisher is healed.

Are you excited for Devil Dinosaur in Marvel Rivals? Share your thoughts on the new hero in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marvel Rivals content, check out the following:

  • How To Play Path To Doomsday: The Avengers Game Mode In Marvel Rivals—Loki Guide
  • How To Claim All Marvel Rivals Path To Doomsday Rewards: The Avengers Event

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: World Fighters Codes (May 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox World Fighters monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Gems, and much more.

World Fighters is a Roblox game that drops players into the middle of a fast-paced anime-inspired combat world filled with enemies to defeat and treasures to discover. Players are required to battle foes and collect valuable Crystals upon launch, and from there, progress through the game to get the best fighters and equipment available. With awakening systems, special abilities, and challenging bosses on offer, the Roblox game has something for everybody.

Players progress by tackling increasingly difficult zones and boss encounters, which drop rare accessories and materials. The game features various fighter units across seven rarity tiers with different attack patterns and special abilities, letting players customize their combat style to their preferences.

Although simply participating in the various battles the game offers is enough for players to keep upgrading their gear, using redeem codes to get some extra resources like Power Potions, Crystal Potions, Damage Potions, Luck Potions, Gems, and special keys is never a bad idea.

Active World Fighters Codes

Expired World Fighters Codes

  • 2MILLIONSSSVISITS
  • EVENT2.5K!

How To Redeem World Fighters Codes

Redeeming World Fighters Codes is relatively easy, just follow the steps listed below to redeem all the active codes:

  1. Boot up World Fighters in Roblox
  2. Click on the shop icon
  3. Scroll all the way to the bottom of the menu
  4. Type the code in the given box
  5. Click on Redeem to claim your freebie

Where To Get More World Fighters Codes

World Fighters codes are typically released through the game’s community and developer channels, but they don’t appear automatically in-game. The best places to catch them early are the official World Fighters Discord server, which has a dedicated codes channel where all new codes are posted first, and the StarX Inc Roblox Group where developers announce updates and milestones.

Codes often come out around game milestones, major updates, and special occasions like holidays, but if you’re only checking sporadically, you’ll very easily miss them because no alert system tells players when a code drops—or when it stops working. Developers typically release codes after major game updates, during holidays and special events, when the game hits community milestones like a certain number of likes or players, and sometimes as compensation when the game experiences server hiccups or bugs.

Because of this, the easiest strategy is to save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself. This page is refreshed as soon as fresh codes go live, and any codes that have expired are taken out quickly so you always see only the ones that still work.

Enjoying our Roblox games codes coverage? Check out the codes for popular games like Steal A Brainrot, Radiant Residents, and Fix It Up on Insider Gaming!

Interested in the latest developments in the world of gaming and esports? Then join the Insider Gaming Discord server and engage with the passionate community.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/roblox-world-fighters-codes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: David Vonderhaar’s BulletFarm Working on New First-Person Multiplayer Game
item.content:encoded:
David Vonderhaar, one of the most legendary developers in the first-person shooter space, has revealed a new initiative taking shape at his studio, BulletFarm Games. The revelation comes alongside news that NetEase pulled funding from BulletFarm amid a bid to divest its Western assets, a move that began last year.

Vonderhaar, who spent almost twenty years at Treyarch before parting ways with the Call of Duty firm in 2023, confirmed that a new holding company, GTG, has committed to supporting BulletFarm going forward. This is empowering the team to create a ‘completely new first-person multiplayer/co-operative experience’.

Vondy is Back

In his first post on social media in over a year, David ‘Vondy’ Vonderhaar explained that the developing game at BulletFarm is a new title but with the same DNA as the project that was shut down when NetEase pulled away from the company.

This was in addition to the BulletFarm profile also sharing a (lengthier) update after a year of radio silence:

The studio is now being backed by GTG (GreaterThan Group), an all-new holding firm established by Simon Zhu, who ironically set up the company after withdrawing from a high-profile role at NetEase. His firm has also backed Arcanaut Studios, which is working on Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic.

In a lengthy report from Bloomberg, it was revealed that GTG has $40 million in the bank and a further $60 million in commitments, ready to deploy. The investment in the likes of Arcanaut and BulletFarm comes from a desire to have everyone work on their dream project:

You can have player satisfaction. You can have commercial success. You can have the achievement of art. You don’t need to compromise or sacrifice one thing for the other. Let’s give the best creator the opportunity to work on a dream project. Everyone wins.

In February 2024, David Vonderhaar unveiled BulletFarm, months after departing from Treyarch. The studio was headquartered in Los Angeles and boasted a line-up of industry veterans, all determined to create an ambitious game in Unreal Engine 5 with a keen focus on building something cooperative.

NetEase dropped the studio after funding the project, and it was left to GTG to keep Vondy’s dreams alive.

Are you eager to see what Vonderhaar’s studio can produce? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Arena Breakout Infinite is getting permanent PvE

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Sony Says Some Chinese and Korean Studios Are Moving Faster Than Western Studios
item.content:encoded:
Sony has commented on the scale and responsiveness of Chinese and Korean studios, comparing them to Western and Japanese studios.

Some interesting deals about Sony and PlayStation are surfacing in a new interview. We have already been informed of the reasons for charging the 30% commission fee, as well as the CEO’s description of PlayStation as a “plastic box without content“. We have now been informed of their opinions on Chinese and Korean Studios.

Sony Says Chinese and Korean Studios Are Showing an Approach Not Seen From Western and Japanese Teams

In a new interview with The Game Business, Christian Svensson, VP of second- and third-party content ventures and strategic initiatives at Sony Interactive Entertainment, discussed the Chinese and Korean studios’ approach. Svensson stated that the pace at which the Chinese and South Korean teams are working is “astonishing.” “The scale and the responsiveness in their approach, it’s something that we’re not necessarily seeing from some of our Western or even Japanese teams.”

Svensson added that the content the teams are receiving from these studios is “very different than the stuff that we’ve seen before, at least on PlayStation.” He also commented on the evolution of these teams and how they are pushing each other. He also spoke about how it is “interesting to see what their trends and trajectories are relative to Western dev communities,” as there is a different dynamic in Chinese and Korean studios.

In other news, Marathon’s failure to deliver has had a massive impact on Sony and Bungie, with a $765 million impairment loss being recorded. Additionally, players might be eligible for a refund from Sony PlayStation following a recent lawsuit settlement. What are your thoughts on Sony’s claim that Chinese and Korean studios are moving faster than Western and Japanese studios? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Cyclops and Devil Dinosaur being confirmed for Marvel Rivals Season 8. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1067)—Answers for Wednesday, May 13
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to another NYT Connections guide! In this handy walkthrough, you’ll find the best NYT Connections hints, answers, and tips anywhere online. In just a matter of seconds, you can smash today’s NYT Connections puzzle with ease, putting an end to any struggles you might be having with it!

Scroll down to get the solutions you need right now!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 13)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • BEAN
  • CROP
  • GRINDER
  • DONUT
  • GROUNDS
  • ADJUST
  • ROLL
  • FILTERS
  • SUB
  • CAUSE
  • BELLY
  • BASIS
  • HERO
  • ARGUMENT
  • MARKUP
  • HOAGIE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words to describe types of sandwiches
  • Green – Something you might use to support your perspective or point
  • Blue – Words you’d associate with editing photographs digitally
  • Purple – Words you can pair nicely with ‘jelly’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 13:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 13 (#1067)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – GRINDER, HERO, HOAGIE, SUB
  • Green – ARGUMENT, BASIS, CAUSE, GROUNDS
  • Blue – ADJUST, CROP, FILTERS, MARKUP
  • Purple – BEAN, BELLY, DONUT, ROLL

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Over 900k Tuned In as Team Ray Wins Jynxzi LoL Tournament
item.content:encoded:
The content creator League of Legends tournament hosted by Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart saw massive viewership.

With 40 streamers playing, including the likes of James ‘Mr Beast’ Donaldson, Ludwig Ahgren, and Mark ‘ohnePixel’ Zimmerman, the tournament hit a combined peak of 908,068 viewers with over 4.6 million hours watched.

This makes it the fourth-most-watched League of Legends tournament in 2026 so far, beating both the LEC and LCS.

Only the LCK and the First Stand international tournament attracted higher viewership.

Team Ray Wins Jynxzi Tournament

The tournament featured six teams led by noobie captains with one Challenger-ranked player on each team forced onto supports with non-damage items.

And in the end, it was the team of Chen-Ruei ‘Ray’ Hsu who came out on top.

He drafted Dutch streamer Alois ‘AloisNL’ Nelissen as his Challenger player and then added Kingsman265, Sapnap, and former Overwatch pro Félix ‘xQc’ Lengyel.

In the final, they met Team Zoil which included Ludwig, ArrowCS, Georgie ‘Pokelawls’ Karam, and former Cloud9 pro Zachary ‘Sneaky’ Scuderi.

The exciting series went to all three games as Jynxzi got his first taste of Silver Scrapes.

Team Ray took the first game with 15 kills for Kingsman’s Mordekaiser and 23 kills for xQc’s Smolder winning them the 43-minute thriller.

Team Zoil then tied things up with a strong deathless performance from his bot lane of Sneaky and Pokelawls, the latter of whom got a penta kill early into the tournament.

But it was xQc who clutched again in the third match. Despite some excellent Nocturne ults by Ludwig, xQc’s Malphite was unkillable with a final score of 12/0/6.

That performance earned Team Zoil the victory and 250 gifted subs for each team member from Jynxzi.

Do you want to see more content creator-focused LoL games and tournaments in the future? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And talking about future events, Korea and KeSPA will participate in the Esports Nations Cup after an initial dispute.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
